extraction.one_line_summary: TEUNS Dylan caught DENNIS Rohan's long-range TT effort in final meters after MCNULTY's early move failed on steep Oron finish
tactical_patterns: DENNIS Rohan's TT power viable for hilltop finishes when launching 2km out, nearly held off pure climbers.
Race leader HAYTER crash at 14km triggered activation sequence; teams waited for chaos before attacking.
MCNULTY Brandon's UAE move failed despite white jersey positioning; team lacked numbers to control steep finale.
INEOS Grenadiers burned DEPLUS extensively throughout stage despite having race leader, tactically questionable energy management.

rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: TT specialists can nearly steal hilltop finishes with 2km attacks when gradient suits power over W/kg
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: negative: lost race lead to crash at 14km, suggesting positioning vulnerability in nervous finale approaches
rider_signals.DEPLUS Laurens: positive: carried exceptional form doing extended pacemaking work for INEOS throughout stage
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: neutral: initiated final climb attack but lacked support to sustain effort through steep gradients
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: positive: timed finale perfectly to benefit from DENNIS's long effort without committing early
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: negative: 5km solo attack gained only 8 seconds despite national champion strength, revealing field's climbing depth
